message:
fix(net/websocket): cancel-safe close retry flushes durable write tail

When a WebSocket close frame is partially written (e.g. only 1 byte hits
the wire before the future is dropped), the connection enters CloseSent
state with the remaining bytes buffered in write_buf. Previously, a
retry of close() would re-initiate the handshake by encoding and sending
a second close frame, corrupting the wire protocol.

Now, initiate_close() in all three WebSocket implementations (client,
server, split) checks for CloseSent state and flushes the existing
write_buf instead of generating a new close frame. This ensures exactly
one close frame reaches the peer regardless of how many times the close
future is cancelled and retried.

Server-side write_buf_to_io() is also reworked: instead of looping
writes to completion (which hides partial-write state from the caller),
it now performs a single write attempt, and if bytes remain, moves them
to the durable write_buf for later flush. This makes the partial-write
path explicit and cancellation-observable.

TestIo gains WriteBehavior::PartialThenPending to simulate partial
writes in tests (writes N bytes on first poll, then returns Pending).

Adds 3 new tests (one per WebSocket variant):
- close_retry_flushes_partially_sent_close_without_second_close (client)
- cancelled_send_after_partial_write_preserves_tail_for_later_flush (server)
- close_retry_flushes_partially_sent_close_without_second_close (server)
- close_retry_flushes_partially_sent_close_without_second_close (split)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
